name: kind-bootstrap-chain
description: Advance the KIND cluster delivery chain. KIND-001a bootstraps the cluster foundation that unblocks the entire PLATFORM delivery sequence. Review confirmations clear the RESTRUCTURE and HA gate backlog.
sprintGoal: KIND-001a passes so KIND-001b and the full PLATFORM chain are unblocked; RESTRUCTURE-001a/b-1 and HA-001 review confirmations are accepted to clear the review pipeline.

stories.title: kind bootstrap: cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh + 3-node cluster + contract/validate.py
stories.description: Split of KIND-001 (achievable=3). Deliver only the cluster scaffolding half: write cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh that creates a 3-node kind cluster named sovereign-test and emits cluster-values.yaml; write contract/validate.py that validates cluster-values.yaml against contract/v1/cluster.schema.yaml. No platform components (Cilium, cert-manager, etc.) — those are KIND-001b.
acceptanceCriteria: shellcheck -S error cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh exits 0
./cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh --dry-run exits 0 and prints intended actions
./cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh runs without error: kind get clusters shows sovereign-test
kubectl get nodes --context kind-sovereign-test shows 3 nodes all Ready
cluster-values.yaml is emitted and python3 contract/validate.py cluster-values.yaml exits 0 with output containing 'CONTRACT VALID'
stories.returnedReason: Split from KIND-001 (achievable=3 — too many integration points for one story)
stories.testPlan: shellcheck -S error cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh; ./cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh --dry-run; ./cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh; kind get clusters; kubectl get nodes --context kind-sovereign-test; python3 contract/validate.py cluster-values.yaml — show all output verbatim.

smart.notes: Achievable score 4: two non-trivial deliverables (bootstrap.sh + validate.py) plus live cluster provisioning in one story; tight but within one Ralph iteration.

stories.title: Expand contract validator test corpus — cover imageRegistry and storageClass invariants
stories.description: contract/validate.py currently has only 2 test fixtures: valid.yaml and invalid-egress-not-blocked.yaml. The validator likely accepts contracts missing imageRegistry or storageClass invariants — there are no test cases to prove otherwise. Add at least 2 more invalid fixtures and verify the validator catches them.
acceptanceCriteria: contract/v1/tests/invalid-no-imageregistry.yaml exists and fails validate.py with a message referencing imageRegistry
contract/v1/tests/invalid-no-storageclass.yaml exists and fails validate.py with a message referencing storageClass
python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/valid.yaml exits 0

G7 gate passes: bash -c 'python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/valid.yaml > /dev/null 2>&1 && python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/invalid-egress-not-blocked.yaml > /dev/null 2>&1 && exit 1 || exit 0' (invalid rejected)
stories.testPlan: Run validate.py against each new fixture and verify exit code and error message. Show output verbatim.

smart.notes: Measurable score 4: AC1 and AC2 describe 'fails validate.py with a message referencing X' but do not provide the exact shell command to verify the message content (e.g., python3 contract/validate.py ... 2>&1 | grep -i 'imageRegistry'). Developer must infer the verification command.

stories.title: Fix docs developer on-ramp — update paths from bootstrap/ and kind/setup.sh to new monorepo structure
stories.description: The monorepo restructure (increment 17) moved kind setup from kind/setup.sh to cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh and charts from charts/ to platform/charts/. Any documentation referencing old paths gives developers incorrect instructions. Scan docs/ and README.md for stale references and update them.
acceptanceCriteria: grep -r 'kind/setup.sh' docs/ README.md returns no matches
grep -r 'bootstrap/providers' docs/ README.md returns no matches
docs/quickstart.md references cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh as the local setup command
markdownlint docs/quickstart.md exits 0
stories.testPlan: Run grep checks above. Show output verbatim.

smart.notes: Measurable score 4: AC3 ('docs/quickstart.md references cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh') is stated as a fact, not as a shell command — needs an explicit grep to be binary verifiable. Relevant score 4: useful T3 hygiene but does not block the KIND-001a → PLATFORM delivery chain; could be deferred without blocking sprint goal.

stories.title: Contract layer: cluster.schema.yaml, validate.py, test fixtures
stories.description: Create contract/v1/cluster.schema.yaml defining the sovereign cluster contract invariants. Create contract/validate.py that validates a cluster config YAML against the schema and exits 1 with an 'AUTARKY VIOLATION' message on constraint failures. Create test fixtures: contract/v1/tests/valid.yaml (passes validation) and contract/v1/tests/invalid-egress-not-blocked.yaml (fails with AUTARKY VIOLATION).
acceptanceCriteria: contract/v1/cluster.schema.yaml exists and is valid YAML: python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('contract/v1/cluster.schema.yaml'))" exits 0
python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/valid.yaml exits 0 with output containing 'CONTRACT VALID'
python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/invalid-egress-not-blocked.yaml exits 1 with output containing 'AUTARKY VIOLATION'
stories.testPlan: python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('contract/v1/cluster.schema.yaml'))" && echo 'schema OK'; python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/valid.yaml; python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/invalid-egress-not-blocked.yaml; show all output verbatim.

stories.title: cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh: kind cluster provisioner with --help, --cluster-name, and kind create cluster invocation
stories.description: Create cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh. The script must: (1) support --help flag that prints usage including '--cluster-name NAME (default: sovereign-test)'; (2) accept --cluster-name NAME option defaulting to 'sovereign-test'; (3) print 'Bootstrapping kind cluster: $cluster_name' before running; (4) invoke 'kind create cluster --name "$cluster_name"'. Script must pass shellcheck -S error with set -euo pipefail at the top. Also verify that bootstrap/providers/ is not present in the working tree (it should have been removed in RESTRUCTURE-001a).
acceptanceCriteria: cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh exists: ls cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh exits 0
shellcheck -S error cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh exits 0
bash -n cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh exits 0
bash cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh --help 2>&1 | grep -q 'cluster-name' exits 0
grep -q 'kind create cluster' cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh exits 0 (script contains the kind invocation)
[ ! -d bootstrap/providers ] exits 0 (bootstrap/providers/ not in working tree)
stories.testPlan: ls cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh && shellcheck -S error cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh && bash -n cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh && bash cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh --help 2>&1 | grep 'cluster-name' && grep 'kind create cluster' cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh && [ ! -d bootstrap/providers ] && echo 'all checks passed'; show all output verbatim

smart.notes: Specific score 4: includes a verification check ([ ! -d bootstrap/providers ]) that belongs to RESTRUCTURE-001a scope, indicating minor scope leakage; all ACs are otherwise well-defined binary shell commands.

stories.title: HA gate script validates all platform charts have replicaCount>=2, PDB, and podAntiAffinity
stories.description: Create scripts/ha-gate.sh that iterates every chart in charts/ and checks: (1) values.yaml has replicaCount >= 2, (2) helm template output contains PodDisruptionBudget, (3) helm template output contains podAntiAffinity. Script exits non-zero and prints FAIL:<chart>:<reason> for each violation. Print PASS:<chart> for each chart that passes all checks. Script must pass shellcheck -S error and support --dry-run flag that lists charts without running helm. IMPORTANT FIX (from sprint-23 review): The grep pipeline for extracting replicaCount must use '|| true' to prevent set -euo pipefail from killing the script when processing charts that have no top-level replicaCount field (e.g. platform/charts/_globals/). Pattern: replica_count=$(grep -E '^replicaCount:' "$chart/values.yaml" || true | awk ...)
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ha-gate.sh exists and passes shellcheck -S error: shellcheck -S error scripts/ha-gate.sh exits 0
bash -n scripts/ha-gate.sh exits 0 (syntax check)
scripts/ha-gate.sh --dry-run prints the list of charts it will check with output containing each chart directory name, then exits 0
For a chart that has replicaCount: 1 in values.yaml, scripts/ha-gate.sh outputs FAIL:<chart>:replicaCount < 2 and exits non-zero
For a chart with replicaCount>=2, PDB, and podAntiAffinity in templates, scripts/ha-gate.sh outputs PASS:<chart> for that chart
stories.testPlan: shellcheck -S error scripts/ha-gate.sh && bash -n scripts/ha-gate.sh && scripts/ha-gate.sh --dry-run; show all output verbatim. Then create a minimal test chart with replicaCount: 1 and run scripts/ha-gate.sh against it to confirm FAIL output.

smart.notes: Specific score 3: description says 'iterates every chart in charts/' but the actual directory is platform/charts/ — inconsistent path reference creates ambiguity for the implementer. Achievable score 3: test plan only covers a synthetic test chart; does not require running against the full platform/charts/ corpus where _globals/ (no replicaCount field) would expose the set -euo pipefail + grep failure mode that the || true fix is meant to solve. Measurable score 4: AC3 ('output containing each chart directory name') is loose — no exact format specified; ACs 4 and 5 use synthetic fixtures rather than the real chart corpus.

stories.returnedReason: bash set -euo pipefail kills script on grep no-match for charts without replicaCount field; fix: add || true guard to grep pipeline (see sprint reviewNotes)
